mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the regime-conditional alpha vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.2% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, generating immediate negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 330, or the threshold effect fails to produce a significant step-change in return distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with stable unemployment rates confirms healthy labor demand, supporting corporate earnings expectations and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where PAYEMS grows >100k and UNRATE remains flat, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines by >0.05 IC.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to improved corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exhibit negative mean direction in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase signals immediate capacity utilization gains, triggering short-term equity rallies within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 102 during recovery regimes, or the signal predictive power disappears after controlling for momentum factors.
